qudipy.dot_array package¶

Submodules¶

qudipy.dot_array.dots module¶

class qudipy.dot_array.dots.DotArray(precalculated_dir, data_dir, preprocessed_dir, n_dots, ctrl_names, ctrl_vals, file_prefix='dots_data', calc_eff=False, save=False, pts=[6, 6, 6])[source]¶

Bases: object

This class is designed to process the potential landscape data set for a given device dot geometry to exctract various meta data or effective parameters.

Example: find the the number of dots for the potential lanscape dataset and then calculate the g-factor deviation for each dot and exchange for all pars of adjacent dots.

exchanges(ctrl_vecs, method='HM')[source]¶

Determines exchange couplings between adjacent pairs of dots.

ctrl_vals: list of lists

Configuration of control voltages for various voltage vectors in the voltage state space

method: string

Choice of ‘HL’, ‘HM’, or ‘LCHO’ method for exchange calculations (strings are case insensitive)

ex_path: 2D float array

Array of the exchange couplings between each adjacent pair of dots for various voltage vectors in the voltage state space

g_factors(ctrl_vecs)[source]¶

Determines g-factors for each dot in the Dots object. Parameters ——- ctrl_vals: list of lists

Configuration of control voltages for various voltage vectors in the voltage state space

g_factor_path: 2D float array

Array of the g-factors of each dot for various voltage vectors in the voltage state space

get_eff_params(exchange_method='HM')[source]¶

Method that calculates the masked potentials and effective parameters for all simulation data. Then interpolation objects are computed.

exchange_method: string

Choice of HL, HM, or LCHO method for exchange calculations

None.

nosingleton(ctrl_vals)[source]¶

Remove single dimensional data

plot(ctrl_vecs, param='gfactor', ex_units='J', yscale='linear')[source]¶
singleton(ctrl_vals)[source]¶

Identify singleton dimensions and return boolean mask

split(group='single')[source]¶

Method that defines new Dots objects where potentials of quantum dots are separated either individually or as pairs.

Each of the new Dots object inherits the potential and electric field data from the parent object, but the interpolating function changes: when called, the potential landscape appears to have only one dot (or one pair)

group: string

Whether to get a list of individual dots (‘single’/’singles’), or a list of adjacent dot pairs (‘pair’/’pairs’)

split_dots: list

list of Dots objects corresponding to individual dots or their pairs
